articles.headline: ASE plans to invest more than US$3.1bn in new fab
articles.summary: ASE Technology Holding plans to invest over US$3.15 billion in a new advanced chip testing facility in Kaohsiung to meet AI-driven customer demand, addressing hardware supply chain bottlenecks. Taiwanese firms like ASE and TSMC face pressure to resolve choke points in global AI supply chains, impacting business operational efficiency and costs.

articles.headline: Samsung’s AI memory boom is rewriting the chip leaderboard
articles.summary: Samsung reported first-quarter profit likely surged over 700% due to demand for high-bandwidth memory chips critical for AI systems, reshaping the semiconductor industry. This reflects growing pricing power in memory for AI training and inference, affecting infrastructure costs for businesses investing in AI. Competitors like SK Hynix and Micron are racing for next-gen HBM4 supply to Nvidia and others.

articles.headline: Ennostar to Ship First AI Modules Amid Restructuring
articles.summary: Taiwan's Ennostar prepares to launch initial AI modules while completing profitable restructuring and investing in optical transceivers. This signals supply chain readiness for AI hardware demand, offering SMEs opportunities in advanced displays and transceivers but tied to chip market volatility.
